mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 8.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that typically precede corporate earnings downgrades, depressing equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 8.0% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and residential housing starts indicates a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate revenue growth, leading to negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint negative signal of falling INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative SPX returns in more than 40% of out-of-sample instances over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment where real GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annually, elevated consumer credit balances amplify consumption-driven earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive skew when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0% and RSAFS expands concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust aggregate demand and wage stability, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive equity returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between monthly PAYEMS changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.05 across all rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 3.5% year-over-year, persistent inflation erodes real consumer purchasing power and forces prolonged monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns over 60 days are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL sustainedly exceeds the 3.5% YoY threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and stagnant industrial production signals a structural demand mismatch that triggers inventory drawdowns and margin compression, depressing equity prices over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and flat or negative INDPRO fails to predict negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 45% of walk-forward instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where industrial production growth outpaces housing starts growth by more than 1.5 percentage points, supply-side efficiency gains dominate demand constraints, yielding positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth exceeds HOUST growth by > 1.5pp
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns do not show a positive mean when INDPRO growth consistently outpaces HOUST growth by the specified margin.
